name: Jimmy Sherman
description: American jazz pianist and arranger, songwriter (1908-1975)
abstract: James Benjamin Sherman (August 17, 1908 in Williamsport, Pennsylvania – October 11, 1975 in Philadelphia) was an American jazz pianist and arranger. Sherman played in dance bands in the late 1920s and played on and off with Jimmy Gorham in the metropolitan Philadelphia area.

about_quotes.speaker: Hayes Pillars
about_quotes.evidence: We had Alphonso Trent, the owner of the band, hired another piano player, a boy named James Sherman, James Sherman joined us and made that tour with us out in -- in Texas.

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah, the rest of them Mack Walker, Bobby Bennett and Clyde Hart, wasn't it, or Sherman. [INTERVIEWER] James Sherman.

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: Oh, we put this boy's name on it, Jimmy Sherman, because he was with the Charioteers, to put it on the Kraft Hall of Fame.

about_quotes.speaker: Jonah Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I think they had Jimmy Sherman, who used to be with the Charioteers, on the piano.

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: Him and Jimmy Sherman. You're going to see Jimmy. He's still alive, I think...
